data.title: Dallas Fed President Advocates Shift in Monetary Policy Approach
data.description:
According to PANews, Dallas Federal Reserve President Lorie Logan has suggested that the Federal Reserve should abandon the federal funds rate as the benchmark for executing monetary policy. Instead, she proposes considering an overnight rate linked to the more stable U.S. Treasury collateral loan market. Logan argues that the federal funds rate target is outdated, with fragile connections between the rarely used interbank market and the overnight money market that could abruptly break. She emphasized that updating the mechanism for implementing monetary policy would be part of an efficient and effective central banking system.

Logan stated, "Some might say that since everything is currently functioning well, there is no need for action. However, if the transmission mechanism between the federal funds rate and other money markets fails, we will need to quickly find an alternative target. I do not believe making significant decisions under time pressure is the best way to promote a strong economic and financial system." She highlighted that the Tri-Party General Collateral Rate (TGCR) could offer the most benefits. Logan noted that TGCR covers over $1 trillion in transactions daily, allowing changes to effectively transmit through the money market. In contrast, the federal funds market currently averages less than $100 billion in trading volume.

data.title: Fed disagreements add to uncertainty surrounding rate cuts, sending Treasury prices down and yields higher.
data.description: U.S. Treasury prices fell as traders digested divergent signals from Federal Reserve officials this week, which have clouded the path of rate cuts. Yields across all maturities rose by about 3 basis points, with the two-year yield, closely tied to Fed policy expectations, rising to 3.60% in New York on Wednesday. Yields on five-year notes remained stable after an auction that saw yields rise by about 0.3 basis points from pre-sale levels. Federal Reserve Chairman Jerome Powell said on Tuesday that the central bank must address both labor market slack and upward inflation risks as it weighs future policy. On Wednesday, Treasury Secretary Benson expressed disappointment, saying Powell had yet to clearly lay out a rate cut agenda. "It's been a bit of a tug-of-war, with disagreements within the committee," said Gregory Faranello, head of U.S. interest rate trading and strategy at AmeriVet Securities. Corporate bond issuance, including one from Oracle, also weighed on Treasury bonds. The software maker plans to raise $18 billion in the U.S. investment-grade bond market on Wednesday, its second-largest bond offering of the year.

data.title: Paxos mints 150 million new PYUSD on Ethereum
data.description: According to Whale Alert monitoring, about 4 hours ago, Paxos minted an additional 150 million PYUSD on Ethereum.

data.title: The probability of the Federal Reserve cutting interest rates by 25 basis points in October is 91.9%.
data.description: According to CME's "Fed Watch," the probability of the Fed keeping interest rates unchanged in October is 8.1%, and the probability of a 25 basis point rate cut is 91.9%. The probability of the Fed keeping interest rates unchanged in December is 1.5%, the probability of a cumulative 25 basis point rate cut is 24.1%, and the probability of a cumulative 50 basis point rate cut is 74.4%.

data.title: Circle issues 250 million more USDC on Solana
data.description: According to Whale Alert monitoring, Circle issued an additional 250 million USDC on the Solana network.

data.title: BlackRock Registers Bitcoin Premium Income ETF in Delaware
data.description: According to PANews, BlackRock has registered the 'iShares Bitcoin Premium Income ETF' in Delaware. The entity is classified as a statutory trust, with BlackRock Advisors, LLC serving as the registered agent. The ETF was established on September 25, 2025, and aims to offer investors premium income related to Bitcoin.

This isn’t the same kind of product as their earlier spot Bitcoin ETF. It’s built to hand out actual income, not track the […]

data.title: Why is the Crypto Market Dumping Today? Bitcoin, Ethereum, and the Fed
data.description:
The cryptocurrency market is experiencing a significant downturn today, raising questions about the underlying reasons for this drastic shift. Notably, major players in the crypto space, such as Bitcoin and Ethereum, are also seeing substantial declines in their values. This fluctuation in the market can be attributed to various factors, including recent developments from the Federal Reserve, which may have influenced investor sentiment and trading strategies. Understanding these dynamics is crucial for anyone looking to navigate the current landscape of cryptocurrency investments effectively.
source: https://blocknews.com/why-is-the-crypto-market-dumping-today-bitcoin-ethereum-and-the-fed/
